title: kage repair: failure engineering — backup-first packet recovery, remediation-first CLI errors
summary: kage repair project <dir json kernel repairProject fixes the four real world breakages in order: 1 unparseable packet JSON in packets/, pending/, and public candidates/ — always backed up to .agent memory/backup/<name .b
body:
`kage repair --project <dir> [--json]` (kernel repairProject) fixes the four real-world breakages in order: (1) unparseable packet JSON in packets/, pending/, and public-candidates/ — always backed up to .agent_memory/backup/<name>.broken first; merge conflicts auto-resolve via splitConflictSides keeping the newest side (updated_at/created_at) only when that side parses as JSON, otherwise the packet is removed loudly with the backup kept; (2) leftover *.tmp/*.lock files under .agent_memory plus daemon/status.json whose pid is dead; (3) missing or stale indexes (catalog packet_count mismatch) rebuilt via indexProject — packet surgery always forces a rebuild; (4) agent wiring drift: setupAgent(write:true) is re-run ONLY for agents whose config exists AND already mentions kage (configMentionsKage) with missing hook scripts — repair must never wire new agents (every Claude Code user has ~/.claude.json; bare config presence is NOT "configured"). Uncaught CLI errors route through remediationFor(error) — a pure, unit-tested heuristic returning exactly one command: ENOENT+.agent_memory→kage init, JSON parse→kage repair, index/graph→kage index, default→kage doctor. kage doctor cross-links "Something broken? kage repair --project ." when validation fails. Tests must pass a temp homeDir to repairProject or they will rewire the developer's real ~/.claude.json.
Evidence: npm test: 207/207 pass including 7 new repair/remediation tests; smoke test: corrupt packet + corrupt catalog.json → kage validate fails with "Try: kage repair --project ." → kage repair fixes both → validate passes
Verified by: node --test dist/kernel.test.js (207 pass) and manual CLI smoke run on a temp project
